reviews.review_text:
My favourite place to stay in Agnes
Really nice bush to camp in , kookaburras to wake you up!
Quick drive to town
Back soon

Lovely and quiet had a kangagroo hop up close to me while setting up we had a yarn then he carried on his merry way back to the bush truth is i only stopped to get some sleep people friendly and great slept like a baby

This beautiful woodland caravan park is just a short drive to town and pet friendly.
The grounds are clean and tidy and offers nicely spaced sections.
They have a short bush walk to Gnome Land, that is great for not just the kids.
The happy hour fire is a great way to meet the owners (who are lovely) and other travellers and just relax with your dri k or choice.
The amenities while older, are kept clean and tidy. As other reviews the showers are on a 5 minute timer, you may need to wait a couple minutes before you can restart if thats what tou need to do, but the showers are very warm and lovely after a day at the beach.
Beautiful quiet site stars are amazing

If travelling to Agnes Waters and 1770, then highly recommend this park. It's tranquil setting and spacious sites, make it preferable to the beach parks, where they pack you in like sardines. The management team are fantastic and although facilities are dated, they're sufficient.

Camping is omringd door bos en daardoor zie je veel vogels. Ruime plekken en vsn van de doorgaande weg naast de camping daar hadden wij geen last van. De douches en wc's zijn wat verouderd maar alles was super schoon. Nog een leuke wandeling gemaakt door het bos en de collectie kabouters tegen gekomen. Vriendelijke eigenaren.
I wish they had a dump point.  Lovely people.

Un camping un peu surprenant, à l'écart du petit village d'Agnes Water.
Les emplacements sont très grands, au milieu des arbres, mais les installations dates un peu (très propre).
Une ambiance un peu "ancienne" flotte sur cet endroit.
Par contre, c'est le camping le moins cher que l'on ait payé pendant les 3 semaines en Australie !!!
Very clean amenities beautiful grass area very quiet and friendly park will be back again for sure
Lovely camp-site, plenty of space.  a bit further from beach than I thought. But tge towns of Agnes Waters and 1770 did not disappointed.
No place to dump, but the location is wonderful. Friendly staff as well.

I didn’t have a great time here, but that was because 1) I had to sleep on the cold hard ground because I could not inflate my mattress, 2) it was cold when I visited: tops of 19 and lows of 10 degrees, 3) I was annoyed by the sound of vehicles going past on the nearby road. But, the campground was OK and it was unusually friendly; I talked with several couples who were passing through and a long-term resident who lent me a hammer to put my pegs in. The owners were friendly and we had a good chat. Forgot to return my keys to the toilet block -remember this if you visit! A convenient and friendly place to stay.

La nostra esperienza non é stata delle migliori. Abbiamo telefonato e ci é stato detto un prezzo di 40$, al nostro arrivo abbiamo pagato 45$. Dopo aver pagato abbiamo chiesto il wi-fi e non era disponibile causa guasto, puó capitare ma essendo una zona non coperta dalla rete (almeno Vodafone) avrei apprezzato ci fosse stato comunicato prima.
I bagni sono veramente datati con il timer di 5 minuti per la doccia… cosa che in un mese di road trip in Australia avevo trovato solo nelle docce gratuite.
Personale non troppo cordiale, il marito della signora in ufficio ha risposto alla mia fidanzata quando ha chiesto di un wifi “se hai Vodafone dimenticati di avere rete”, ridendo.

La cucina é ben fornita, bbq, gas, lavandino con acqua calda, tostapane, micronde, bollitore acqua e tavoli coperti.

Le piazzole alimentate (+aqua) sono molto larghe.
Nice level big caravan areas, clean facilities. Only drawback is no dump point, you have to drive quite a few km to empty canister.

Super accueil ça fait plaisir, le camping est trop chouette, les emplacements sont grands, les sanitaires sont propres et la camp kitchen aussi. À 8 minutes de la ville et de la plage, bon rapport qualité prix.

Great place pretty much in the middle of a small rainforest and nice and quiet. 10mins from the main beach by car but still close enough to see and do everything....
Shower amenities are a bit dated but adequate.
8 kms from town. Huge sites. Very old amenities but kept clean. Would stay again.
Big sites. Quite. Only one problem not much tv and phone reception

Honestly worst camping trip ever!!!|1. Don’t be fooled by the amtiputhetre area on their website and the football because it certainly looks nothing like it, all weeds over grown, electrical cords everywhere. Photos attached |2. Knomieland we didn’t even make it that far after walking through the bush of long grass/ sticks for 20mins we gave up following the path to it. |3. The pool shade awning is half hanging down and held by ratchet straps.|4. The showers, they have disabled yeah sweet for a family with young children to go in and there’s no shower head attached they have removed it, yeah sweet go into females turn water on and they’re on timers of 5mins, took almost 3 whole minutes to get the water hot, I get in with the kids get wet and start soaping up, water goes off. Have to wait a whole 5 mins before it comes back, by this time kids are crying, covered in soap, cold and when it does finally come back on another 2-3 mins to get hot water again and then constantly changing from cold to boiling hot|5. The old couple that run the place are the rudest people I have ever met in my life to myself, my partner and even our children.|We had a few nights booked in and left after 1|I will not be returning
Lovely quiet caravan park. Very clean amenities.
Nice friendly camp site .Special thanks to Lee for warm welcoming while we are stressing about finding a campsite after 7pm.
It was lovely and quiet here set amongst bush with kangaroos and lots of birdlife. Great value for money and only a short drive into Agnes Water. No need to pay top dollar for 'beachside' parks.
Amenities are old and grounds are not well maintained. Staff nice. Value for money.
You could hear the banjo’s
We ha

If you want your little piece of heaven in the bush while you need the water close this is the place to be
We been travelling for 3 months and this is one of our favourite caravan park to be in
From the beautiful owners that are so kind and welcoming to the atmosphere in the place and of course all the beautiful wild life and greenery.
we are so grateful to find this place and highly recommended it!
Thank you for everything!

Good location clean amenities shops nearby
Friendly staff

Can I give zero stars??

Actually, the bush view in the unpowered section was nice, and the kids liked the walk to the gnome garden. But they are the only positives. Oh and we saw a lizard..

Management skills and customer service: non existent.

$48/night - NO POWER, NO WATER!
($30 for 2 adults, $9 each for 2 of the kids!)

When we booked with Phil we repeatedly told him clear as day we had a 12m bus towing a car on a trailer, but upon arrival the lady (wife I'm guessing) defensively tells me they have nothing on our booking about the size of our vehicle... They then tell us we now won't fit on the spot they had put us on (next to our friends' caravan [even though we would have]) so put us in a spot in the back corner. After driving all day with 4 kids we check-in, at 5pm, almost in the dark, down a narrow track with overhanging branches which scratched my bus.
The next day we realise we are under almost 100% tree cover and will get almost no solar (hence unpowered section, to save money right..) so had to run our generator almost every day (2 weeks). Powered section is in full sun...Ironic.
We did manage to move after 2 weeks to a site with sun, for 1 more week.

The worst part was despite there being taps everywhere, there is no water in the unpowered section! I think this is by design.  Apparently they were "being fixed" when we booked, yet still weren't fixed when we left, some 3 months or so later... hmm

The day after check-in I asked about filling from the tank with the tap on the side, about 60m from our site, to which she said "no, no one is allowed to touch that" yet when she turned and asked him he said "yeah no problem"  ...weird.
So I did. I connected my hoses to theirs, which was already connected to the tap, had to use my car battery to run a 12v pump, and pumped it into my tanks. When Phil came over and saw me doing this he smugly said "you wont get much water out of there." and I said "well, I am, it's going in now" and his reply was "mmm well that's sneaky.." implying that I was doing something wrong.

Every day since that day, that tap no longer worked....
Our only option then was to go and fill up our bladder/jerry can in the powered section, drive/carry it back, and pump/tip it into our tanks. I can assure you, after 2 or 3 weeks this gets very old.

To add insult to injury, after 2 weeks of getting our own water I saw Phil and asked him about turning that tap at the tank on, to which he said they were going on holiday that day, but would, reluctantly, turn the tap on for me for about an hour (this sounds like a long time, but pumping gravity only water, through a 12v pump takes a long time). As promised he left at 12:00 and the tap was then off, without being enough time to get much water.

When I went to speak with the new caretaker about possibly turning the tap back on or letting me turn it off later or something, I was met with the rudest woman I have ever met. She kicked me out of the office for simply asking a few questions. Yes I may have gotten a bit agitated, but this was in defence of being treated so poorly. Apparently paying customers are not important to this place.

The new caretaker's husband then tells us later that the owners are "being quite generous" with the water.. haha yeah ok.

No dump point, their bins were often overflowing, the washing machine left our clothes worse than when we put them in, and the outdated showers have 5 minute timers on them.

For this level of service, with no power or water, these sites are worth maybe $15-$20/night, not $48.
If you charge for kids, you need a playground or a warm pool. Luckily mine are happy playing with sticks and their own toys.

Finally, on their 2 pages (yes, 2 pages) of rules, they ask you not to give bad reviews, but to come and speak with them to resolve any issues in person... haha yep well, tried that. Now you get a review.

Go on, reply defensively and try to justify everything I just said, like you did with all the other bad reviews you have.
Don't bother, I won't read it
The park was lovely and quiet but that amenities need an upgrade.

My second short stay here. Whilst priced keenly compared to other local parks I do have concerns to air here.|Tried calling day before but could not get thru. Finally connected only to be informed a power pole fire had cut phone,internet. Rather than apologise for any inconvenience the manager was quite defensive and clearly agitated,unwelcoming.Wanted payment for one night upfront. On arrival at park manager was still agitated and unwelcoming with no hospitality shown at all. My partner endured a hot shower with no running cold water. Showers are on a timer although not necessary really. Only one powered appliance can be used in camp kitchen at a time. No tv reception and limited phone signal. Sites are large in bush setting. Showers are quote dated.

Great caravan park powered and Un powered good amenities. Owners put fire pit on Monday nights and Friday night plenty gather around chatting.
Park and drive into town.
NO groceries at reception buy first.
Great for big rigs too. Like mine.
Thank you for welcome stay.

Hidden gem of a place. We initially were to stay 2 nights but extended.

Park is impeccably kept, the owners made us feel at home and we loved that our dogs could come too.

Basic house rules were appreciated, fire every night helped with community. Everybody were friendly.

Loved the birdykins, the spacious unpowered site we stayed on and amenities cleaned.

We would choose this over any other caravan park in Agnus. It was peaceful and close enough to the beach without being in the madness of the tourist hotspot.

Thank you for accommodating us on short notice and we will be back soon for longer.

Toyah & Darren
Good campsites. Ammenities are clean.
Awesome place to stay short notice
This caravan park is delightful. Big clean sites, clean tidy amenities, lovely gardens and great bush walk for the dog.
A great caravan park, close to town. Clean amenities,  friendly people.

Amazing caravan park - spacious sites, super clean amenities, surrounded by nature, super cool fire pit for groups and just a 5-minute drive to Agnes Water.

Plenty of activities also to get to meet other people, just a pity that we stayed only one night and couldn’t attend them.

The owners were also lovely and super helpful!

I don’t want to tell, because you’ll all go there. Anyhow it’s a very friendly place and it’s like camping used to be before the big corporations moved in. I reserve 5 stars for places with flowers in the private bathrooms 🙂. I’ve stayed at a lot of caravan parks and this is one of only two  I would go out of my way to return to.
Lovely quiet place. Clean toilets great host.

The reef has been our home for the past month and we have thoroughly enjoyed our stay here whilst working in 1770. There is nothing more could you ask for from a caravan park: spacious sites, clean amenities, friendly owners, interesting wildlife and a great location. We have made friends for life here and we will miss our reef family. Thank you so much for making our stay so relaxed and fun Lee and Bill.

We will miss you all 🥰

We stayed here with our Labrador 'Luna' for 3 nights.
The gardens were well maintained and considering the drought conditions of area, presented beautifully. We found the owner(Bill) reasonable and accommodating. We had a community camp fire and Pizza and Pasta night. Bill stayed after lighting the fire and had great yarn with us. The park is just out of Agnes Waters at Round Hill but an easy drive to Agnes Water Central and 1770. We would definitely recommend and return to stay.

A small rural haven with spacious sites. Great swimming pool. Quiet and not brightly lit so it is peaceful. Timed showers, with which we had no problem and the amenities are older but scrupulously clean. Fires allowed and firewood available. Wouldn’t hesitate to stay again and the caretaker (Regina) was a hoot! Thoroughly enjoyed our stay.

Nice little park, old style, quiet and grass sites. Amenities could be better
$45 pn.

Wow ❣️ We have just had the best week here at the Reef CP. Beautiful Gardens 🌴, so much love & hard work has gone into the park 🌺 Our site was spacious & easy to access. Clean amenities 🌟 Friendly Fun evenings around the communal Fire Pit 🔥 Awesome Place to relax, kick back & enjoy nature with all the beaches, shops just up the road.
Thanks to Bill & Leigh & Team for an awesome stay. We will be back 🤩

The up side:  Old but clean amenities. Sites are large and afford some privacy. The drive to Agnes water is anly 6km, on good sealed road. The park  is very peaceful and in a lovely bush setting.||The down side:  Mobile reception is marginal, only one bar on my site. No TV reception but they do supply a cable link for a modest hire price. Even then, reception is marginal.  NO DUMP POINT. The nearest being at 1770 SES ground.  The No Refund policy is quite usual with caravan parks but in this case, where many people who arrive without a booking, are turned away, It is not justified.  There is no doubt that if you leave early, the park owners will make double on your site.

Awesome caravan park, huge sites which are easy to get into. Very clean facilities. Surrounded by trees and so peaceful. Lovely managers who are very helpful. Only 5 minutes into Agnes Water and surrounds. So pleased we stayed there.

Quiet environment away from the busy part of Agnes Water

A haven in which to relax and recover
Surrounded by trees, large sites, good clean amenities, good Camp Kitchen, friendly,helpful staff. Peaceful and quiet , not too close to the growing metropolis of Agness Waters and 1770.
Dump Point is 12 kilometres away at 1770...mmm

Wasn't impressed with the way the female "manager " went psycho at me because I entered the park without checking in first. NEWSFLASH if there was more room to prop out the front I could follow the Nazism and check in first.
Great van park with good size spots, well defined. Amenities older but neat an clean. Happy people.

Stayed here 2 nights in our 24' Gooseneck.|The amenities are old, but cleaned daily. |The spaces are large, which we loved, weren't squashed in on top of each other as some parks are. |It was 8 min to Agnes Water and 1770.|Really lovely quiet spot.|The showers are on a 5min timer, conserving the tank water, not a problem for us as we live on Tank water too. But city folk should take note.|The office has some basic supplies like milk and gas for your BBQ bottles.|We enjoyed our stay here and would really like to see some TLC done on the amenities building.|We paid $45/night July 2023.

DO NOT STAY HERE
-rundown amenities with a 5 minute timer on the hot water in the shower
-tv connections do not work therefore no tv
- no radio reception
- poor internet reception
- over 6kms from the heart of Agnes Water
